content.content:
content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Card Replacement | Chase
- url:\
  \ https://www.chase.com/digital/replace-card
- source: Chase
- domain: chase.com
\
  
## Content

# Request a new Chase card

## Card replacement

It’s easy\
  \ to replace a lost or misplaced card.

### On the Chase Mobile\xAE app

1.\
  \ Sign in to your account in the mobile app.
2. Tap “More.”
3. Tap “\
  Replace cards.”
4. Select the card you need to replace and reason for replacement.
\
  5. Confirm your request and click “Replace card.”

### On chase.com
\
  
1. Sign in and click on any of your checking accounts or credit cards.
2. Click\
  \ “More,” “Account services” and “Replace a card.”\
  
3. Select the card to replace.
4. Select the reason for replacement.
5. Confirm\
  \ your request and click “Replace card.”

## Manage your debit card\
  \ all in one place

At the debit card help center, you can activate, lock or unlock\
  \ your card and change your PIN.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### What do\
  \ I do if my card is stolen?

If you think someone used your debit card without\
  \ your permission, call us right away at 1-800-935-9935 for help.

If you think\
  \ someone used your credit card without your permission, call us right away at 1-800-955-9060\
  \ for help.

### How do I request a replacement card if my card is lost, damaged\
  \ or no longer works?

To request a replacement card, sign in to Chase Online℠\
  \ and follow the instructions. We’ll send you a replacement card that'll arrive\
  \ within 5-7 business days.

If your card doesn't arrive after 7 business days,\
  \ sign in, choose "Didn't receive card" under "Reason" and follow the instructions.\
  \ You can also call the number on your statement.

### What do I do if my credit\
  \ card has or is about to expire?

If your account is in good standing and the\
  \ credit card is about to expire, Chase will automatically send you a new card during\
  \ the month your current card will expire. If you haven’t received a new one,\
  \ call the number listed on your statement or the back of your card.
"

content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Debit Card Help Center | Chase
\
  - url: https://www.chase.com/personal/checking/debit-card-help
- source: Chase
\
  - domain: chase.com

## Content

# Debit Card Help Center

## Tools to help\
  \ manage your card

### Activate

Activate now, call 1-800-290-3935 or use any\
  \ Chase ATM.

### Replace

Use the Chase Mobile\xAE app or chase.com to get\
  \ a new card.

### Lock/Unlock

Lost or misplaced your debit card? It’\
  s easy to temporarily lock it.

### Change PIN

Just call the number on the\
  \ back of your card.

## Using your debit card can be safer than cash

### Zero\
  \ Liability Protection

Unauthorized debit card transactions are reimbursed when\
  \ reported promptly.

Report fraud.

### Fraud Monitoring

Your debit card\
  \ includes fraud monitoring — Chase may notify you of unusual debit card purchases.
\
  
## Your debit card gives you more convenient ways to pay

### Tap to pay

\
  Tap your debit card on the checkout terminal and pay without swiping or inserting\
  \ your card.

### Digital wallet

Add your debit card to your digital wallet\
  \ and you can make purchases with your phone.

## Don’t have a Chase checking\
  \ account?

Find the account that's right for you and start enjoying these benefits.
\
  
Chase Mobile\xAE app is available for select mobile devices. Message and data\
  \ rates may apply. Screen images are simulated; actual appearance may vary.
"

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Chase Customer Service | Chase

  - url: https://www.chase.com/digital/customer-service

  - source: Chase

  - domain: chase.com


  ## Content


  # Chase Customer Service


  ## Self-service actions


  Get the banking services help you need with Chase Customer Service. Chase helps
  customers find answers to questions.


  Self-service actions include:


  - Make a payment

  - Order checks

  - Manage account alerts

  - Replace a lost/damaged card

  - Dispute a charge

  - Reset username/password

  - Find account/routing number

  - Report fraud

  - Payment support

  - Search more topics


  ## Get help with your account


  Chase provides tools and information to guide customers for:


  - Credit Cards

  - Personal Banking

  - Investment & Retirement Planning

  - Chase Private Client

  - Auto Loans

  - Home Lending

  - Commercial Banking

  - Business Banking


  ## Frequently Asked Questions


  ### Credit Card


  Card Customer Service: 1-800-432-3117.


  ### Personal Banking


  Personal Banking Customer Service: 1-800-935-9935.


  Answers about checking and savings accounts, and debit cards are available through
  the personal banking help area.


  ## Still have questions?


  If you need to contact Chase, Chase Customer Service can help.


  Customer help and technical support includes online and mobile banking support,
  or help with your account.


  Call Chase Customer Service: 1-800-935-9935.


  Chase also accepts operator relay calls.


  ## Connect with us on social media


  Tweet @ChaseSupport or message Chase on Facebook or Instagram.


  Please don''t include account numbers or personal information.


  ## Schedule a meeting


  Meet with a banker to discuss your account or open a new one.

  '

title: Chase Customer Service | Chase
url: https://www.chase.com/digital/customer-service

Your Chase debit card ending in **4826** is marked as **damaged and not working**. The account is open and in good standing, with no fraud alert or unauthorized-transaction issue. Since you still have the card, you should request a **replacement for a damaged/nonworking card**—not report it lost or stolen.

**Next step:**
1. Sign in to the **Chase Mobile app**.
2. Tap **More → Replace cards**.
3. Select the debit card and choose the damaged/nonworking reason.
4. Confirm the replacement.

You can also use Chase.com: select your checking account, then **More → Account services → Replace a card**. Chase says replacement cards generally arrive within **5–7 business days**. If you prefer help by phone, call Chase Personal Banking at **1-800-935-9935**.

Until the replacement arrives, you may be able to use the card through a digital wallet if it is already added. Don’t share your full card number or PIN here.

Sources: [Chase Card Replacement](https://www.chase.com/digital/replace-card), [Chase Debit Card Help Center](https://www.chase.com/personal/checking/debit-card-help)

final_answer:
Your Chase debit card ending in **4826** is marked as **damaged and not working**. The account is open and in good standing, with no fraud alert or unauthorized-transaction issue. Since you still have the card, you should request a **replacement for a damaged/nonworking card**—not report it lost or stolen.

**Next step:**
1. Sign in to the **Chase Mobile app**.
2. Tap **More → Replace cards**.
3. Select the debit card and choose the damaged/nonworking reason.
4. Confirm the replacement.

You can also use Chase.com: select your checking account, then **More → Account services → Replace a card**. Chase says replacement cards generally arrive within **5–7 business days**. If you prefer help by phone, call Chase Personal Banking at **1-800-935-9935**.

Until the replacement arrives, you may be able to use the card through a digital wallet if it is already added. Don’t share your full card number or PIN here.

Sources: [Chase Card Replacement](https://www.chase.com/digital/replace-card), [Chase Debit Card Help Center](https://www.chase.com/personal/checking/debit-card-help)
